allow(bytes32,address).details: The caller must be allowed to use handle for allow() to succeed. If not, allow() reverts.

allowTransient(bytes32,address).details: The caller must be allowed to use handle for allowTransient() to succeed.                      If not, allowTransient() reverts.                      The Coprocessor contract can always allowTransient(), contrarily to allow().

cleanTransientStorage().details: This function removes the transient allowances, which could be useful for integration with      Account Abstraction when bundling several UserOps calling the FHEVMExecutor Coprocessor.

params.contractAddress: The contract address forming the user decryption context.
params.expirationDate: UNIX timestamp when the delegation expires.

params.contractAddress: The contract address forming the user decryption context.

returns._0: isAccountDenied  Whether the account is on the deny list.
isAllowed    Whether the account can access the handle.
isDecryptable    Whether the handle can be publicly decrypted.

params.contractAddress: The contract address to delegate access to.
params.delegate: The address of the account that receives the delegation.
params.delegator: The address of the account that delegates access to its handles.
params.handle: The handle to check for delegated user decryption.

returns._0: isDelegatedForUserDecryption Whether the handle can be accessed for delegated user decryption.

params.data: Array containing the ABI-encoded function calls.

returns.results: Return payloads for each call in `data`.

params.contractAddress: The contract address forming the user decryption context.

allow(bytes32,address).notice: Allows the use of handle for the address account.

allowForDecryption(bytes32[]).notice: Allows a list of handles to be decrypted.

allowTransient(bytes32,address).notice: Allows the use of handle by address account for this transaction.

delegateForUserDecryption(address,addres.notice: Delegates user decryption rights to `delegate` for the specified `contractAddress`.

getUserDecryptionDelegationExpirationDat.notice: Returns the expiration date for delegated user decryption rights.

isAccountDenied(address).notice: Returns whether the account is on the deny list.

isAllowed(bytes32,address).notice: Returns whether the account is allowed to use the handle, either due to                      allowTransient() or allow().

isAllowedForDecryption(bytes32).notice: Returns wether a handle is allowed to be publicly decrypted.

isHandleDelegatedForUserDecryption(addre.notice: Returns whether an account is delegated to access the handle for user decryption.

multicall(bytes[]).notice: Executes a batch of encoded calls on the ACL contract.

persistAllowed(bytes32,address).notice: Returns whether the account is persistently allowed to use the handle.

revokeDelegationForUserDecryption(addres.notice: Revokes previously delegated user decryption rights.
